intro: Dortmanna flexuosa, also known as Marsh St. John's Wort, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Primulaceae family, characterized by its yellow flowers and slender stems. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat anxiety, insomnia, and mild depression, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Bioactive constituents of Dortmanna flexuosa include iridoids, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Marsh St. John's Wort include teas, tinctures, and infusions, often used to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
